Barre workouts are great for pregnancy and postpartum because they’re relatively low-impact and safe for', '5. Boat Pose (Roller Boats)
Targets:\xa0The deep hip flexors (which can be weak from sitting too much), abs and core.

How To Do Roller Boats

Sit on the mat on your sit bones, legs bent at 90 degrees, feet flat. Tilt your hips forward, rounding through the spine as you pull your navel towards your spine (making a ‘C’ shape with your upper body). Keep your shoulders down and the crown of your head towards the ceiling.
Clasp your hands behind your thighs and roll onto your back, then return to your sit bones.
Take a moment to balance and reset posture. Then, if you’re able to, release your hands from the back of your legs and extend your legs out away from your body, toes pointed.
Hold the extended position for a moment. response:
To do the Boat Pose (Roller Boats) in a barre-inspired workout:

1. Sit on the mat on your sit bones with your legs bent at 90 degrees and feet flat.
2. Tilt your hips forward, rounding through the spine as you pull your navel towards your spine, creating a ‘C’ shape with your upper body.
3. Keep your shoulders down and the crown of your head towards the ceiling.
4. Clasp your hands behind your thighs and roll onto your back, then return to your sit bones.
5. Take a moment to balance and reset your posture.
6. If able, release your hands from behind your legs and extend your legs out away from your body with toes pointed.
7. Hold the extended position briefly.
8. Place your hands behind your thighs and repeat the roll.

user_input: How do the pregnancy-friendly workouts in the 20-Minute Barre Class incorporate exercise modifications for different skill levels to ensure safety and effectiveness?
retrieved_contexts:
['Jump to Workout

Barre meets pilates, yoga sculpt and strength training in this 20-Minute Barre Class At Home!
If you’ve done any of my barre, pilates or yoga workouts, you know that I love putting my own personal spin on traditional classes by adding larger, full range exercises and heart-pumping cardio.
If you don’t normally do barre classes, I encourage you to try this one. This 20-Minute Barre Class will challenge you in new ways. The tiny pulsing movements are harder than they look!
Plus, I filmed this barre class workout round 16 weeks pregnant so it’s pretty pregnancy-friendly. Of course, you know your body best and should modify as needed for your own pregnancy.', '20-Minute Barre Class At Home

We’ll combine classic barre moves with strength training and cardio in this dynamic barre workout.
This workout includes smaller, pulsing movements to tighten and tone hard-to-hit muscles groups, as well as larger, full-body exercises to increase fat burning benefits for weight loss.
Add this barre workout routine to your beginner or\xa0pregnancy exercise program\xa01-2 times a week to maintain strength and endurance.
Workout Equipment:
Optional set of light weights (3-5 lbs). I am using a set of 5 lb dumbbells in this barre workout. You could also use your bodyweight.
Option to add a chair/countertop for extra balance support.', 'they’re relatively low-impact and safe for joints. Barre is also a great way to rebuild core and pelvic floor strength postpartum. You’ll find some barre-inspired exercises for postpartum core recovery here.  How Can I Modify My Barre Class During Pregnancy? Barre workouts can involve intense abdominal work and deep twists through the spine, which should be avoided as your pregnancy progresses.\xa0I filmed this barre workout when I was about 16 weeks pregnant. Women in their second and third trimesters of pregnancy (or those who are newly postpartum) may need to modify the plank and core work at the beginning of the workout video.\xa0Alternatively, you might try this 25-Minute Prenatal Barre Workout or this 3rd Trimester Pregnancy Barre Workout.', 'Workout Plans

response:
Barre Pilates offers suitable workout modifications for pregnancy and prenatal fitness by avoiding intense abdominal work and deep spinal twists as pregnancy progresses. For women in their second and third trimesters or those newly postpartum, modifications are recommended, especially for plank and core exercises. Alternative workouts such as a 25-Minute Prenatal Barre Workout or a 3rd Trimester Pregnancy Barre Workout are suggested to accommodate these needs. Many classes also provide modifications for different fitness levels, including pre- and post-natal participants with medical approval.

Beginners can expect Barre Pilates classes to be guided by certified instructors who lead participants through slow, precise, and controlled movements using the ballet barre for balance. The workout structure focuses on activating the core, improving posture, and building muscle endurance through small pulses. Equipment typically used includes the ballet barre and light props such as resistance bands or small weights. The low-impact, steady-paced nature of Barre Pilates makes it accessible and safe for beginners, with instructors offering variations to accommodate different fitness levels and physical abilities.
reference: Barre Pilates combines ballet-inspired movements with Pilates principles, emphasizing slow, precise, and controlled exercises that activate the core, pelvic floor, and smaller stabilizing muscles. This low-impact workout is suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and those with joint concerns, because it uses the ballet barre for balance and support and incorporates light props such as resistance bands, small weights, and yoga mats. Classes typically last 45 to 60 minutes and include warm-up, targeted muscle group exercises, core work on the mat, and a cool-down with stretching. For pregnancy and prenatal fitness, Barre Pilates offers modifications to accommodate the changing needs of expectant mothers, such as avoiding intense abdominal work and deep spinal twists as pregnancy progresses. Certified instructors provide safe variations and may suggest using a chair or countertop for extra balance support. The workout strengthens the core and pelvic floor, which is beneficial during and after pregnancy. Beginners can expect a supportive environment with clear guidance on form and alignment, making Barre Pilates an accessible and effective option for improving muscle tone, balance, posture, and overall fitness during pregnancy.

user_input: How do plank leg lifts and fire hydrant kicks in the 10-Minute Barre Core Workout target the gluteus maximus and core muscles through low-impact high-intensity movements, and how does this relate to the overall benefits of barre core workouts for muscle tone and posture?

This barre core workout includes some of my favorite barre-inspired exercises to strengthen your abs and glutes.
I’m all about non-traditional core training. I believe the best abdominal exercises\xa0train your core to do what it is intended to do — stabilize your torso as your limbs move away from your body. That’s why I like to include a variety of exercises in my core workouts.

Plank Leg Lifts
Targets:\xa0Shoulders, triceps, glutes, lower back, abs and core.

How To Do Plank Leg Lifts

Start in a high plank position with\xa0hands shoulder-width apart. Your body should be in a straight line from head to toes, gaze slightly in front of you.
From this plank position, engage your core as you use your glutes and hamstrings to\xa0raise your right leg off the floor until it’s at about hip height. Foot should be flexed.
Hold at the top for a moment then,\xa0lower your right\xa0leg back to the floor with control.

Modification:\xa0Perform a table top leg lift instead of a plank leg lift (with the opposing left leg bent on the ground to support you).

2. Fire Hydrant Kicks
Targets:\xa0Gluteus maximus, hip abductors and core.

How To Do Fire Hydrant Kicks', 'By: Lindsey Bomgren, CPT | March 28, 2025

Jump To Workout']
response:
Plank leg lifts and fire hydrant kicks in the 10-Minute Barre Core Workout target the gluteus maximus and core muscles by engaging these areas through controlled, low-impact, high-intensity movements. 

- **Plank Leg Lifts**: Starting in a high plank position, you engage your core to stabilize your torso while lifting one leg using the glutes and hamstrings. This movement challenges the core to maintain stability as the limb moves away from the body, activating the abs, lower back, and glutes simultaneously.

- **Fire Hydrant Kicks**: This exercise specifically targets the gluteus maximus and hip abductors while also engaging the core to maintain balance and control during the movement.

These exercises exemplify barre’s focus on small, precise movements that dig deep into muscles, emphasizing stability and control. By continuously engaging the core and glutes in this way, barre workouts build lean muscle tone and improve muscle definition.

The overall benefits of barre core workouts include enhanced posture and core stability, as they activate deep abdominal muscles, pelvic floor, and back muscles that support the spine. Borrowing alignment principles from ballet, barre workouts teach lengthening of the spine and proper body positioning, which improves balance, stability, and posture while reducing back pain. Thus, plank leg lifts and fire hydrant kicks contribute directly to these benefits by strengthening the core and glute muscles through low-impact, high-intensity movements that promote muscle tone and better posture.
reference: In the 10-Minute Barre Core Workout, plank leg lifts and fire hydrant kicks are key exercises that target the gluteus maximus and core muscles using low-impact, high-intensity movements. Plank leg lifts involve starting in a high plank position and lifting one leg to about hip height, engaging the glutes, hamstrings, shoulders, triceps, lower back, abs, and core. This exercise emphasizes core stability as the body maintains a straight line while the leg lifts, promoting muscle tone and balance. Fire hydrant kicks are performed from a tabletop position, focusing on the gluteus maximus, hip abductors, and core by lifting the knee in line with the hip and then extending the leg. Both exercises incorporate controlled, barre-inspired movements that enhance muscle engagement without high impact, making them suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and those with joint concerns. These movements align with the overall benefits of barre core workouts, which emphasize stability, control, and strengthening of smaller, stabilizing core muscles. Barre workouts improve muscle tone, balance, and posture by challenging the core to stabilize the torso as limbs move away from the body, reducing injury risk and enhancing functional strength.

response:
The NML App provides all workout programs and coaching in one place, making it convenient for a busy fitness enthusiast to access barre-inspired workouts that require no equipment. These workouts focus on low-impact, high-intensity movements designed to strengthen the core and tone trouble areas like thighs, glutes, arms, and triceps, while also improving posture by engaging the core to stabilize the body during movement.

The 10-minute barre core workout is a mat-based ab workout that emphasizes barre-inspired movements challenging the core to stabilize as limbs move away from the body. It targets smaller, stabilizing core muscles often missed in full-body workouts, helping to build lean core strength efficiently in a short time.

In contrast, the 20-minute barre class at home is a longer session that likely incorporates a broader range of barre movements and possibly targets additional muscle groups beyond the core, offering a more comprehensive barre workout experience.

Thus, the NML App supports busy individuals by providing flexible workout durations and focused barre workouts that improve core strength and posture without the need for equipment.
reference: The NML App provides accessible barre-inspired workouts designed to improve core strength, balance, and posture, fitting well into a busy schedule. The 10-minute barre core workout is a no-equipment, mat-based routine featuring five barre-inspired ab exercises that target the glutes, obliques, upper abs, and lower abs. It focuses on small, controlled movements to stabilize the torso and strengthen the core, suitable for performing 1-2 times a week to build muscle and improve abdominal definition. In contrast, the 20-minute barre class combines traditional barre exercises with cardio bursts, yoga sculpt, pilates, and strength training. It includes both small pulsing movements and larger full-body exercises to increase intensity and fat burning, making it a dynamic workout that also supports pregnancy fitness. This class optionally uses light weights and can incorporate a chair or countertop for balance support. Both workouts are led by certified personal trainer Lindsey Bomgren and are available on the NML App, offering flexible options for core strengthening and posture improvement without the need for equipment.
